connections.gloss: occasion to fall (of stumbling), offence, thing that offends, stumblingblock
connections.def: a trap-stick (bent sapling), i.e. snare (figuratively, cause of displeasure or sin)
from 3895; a side-slip (lapse or deviation), i.e. (unintentional) error or (wilful) transgression:--fall, fault, offence, sin, trespass. see GREEK for

from 1 (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of 4350; actively, inoffensive, i.e. not leading into sin; passively, faultless, i.e. not led into sin:--none (void of, without) offence. see…

connections.gloss: caused to fall, offence, [idiom] (no-) thing offered, ruin, stumbling-block.
